shortDescription: Male breast reduction in Korea — Seoul surgeons combine liposuction with gland excision for a flatter, firmer chest, typically $3,000–$9,000.
quickAnswer: Gynecomastia surgery removes excess fat and **glandular tissue** from the male chest, usually through **liposuction combined with direct gland excision** via small incisions.
In Korea most procedures take **1–2 hours**, are done as **day surgery or with one overnight stay**, and use general or tumescent local anesthesia.
Typical Korea pricing runs **about $3,000–$9,000 USD** depending on whether liposuction alone or a combined gland-excision technique is needed.
Plan **7–10 days in Korea** for the procedure, suture removal, and an early follow-up before flying home.

keyFacts.label: Anesthesia
keyFacts.value: General, or tumescent local anesthesia with sedation

keyFacts.label: Hospital stay
keyFacts.value: Day surgery or one overnight stay

keyFacts.label: Recovery
keyFacts.value: Back to desk work in ~1 week; full activity and exercise by 4–6 weeks

sections.overview:
Gynecomastia is the enlargement of male breast tissue caused by excess fat, glandular growth, or both. It is common and benign, but can affect chest contour and confidence.

Surgical correction removes the surplus tissue to restore a flatter, firmer chest. The right approach depends on whether the bulk is mostly fat, mostly gland, or a mix.

In Korea, gynecomastia surgery is offered widely in Seoul, especially the **Gangnam** district, where many clinics run dedicated international-patient services.

Most cases are treated with a **combination technique**:

- **Liposuction** to remove excess fatty tissue and refine the contour
- **Direct excision** of the firm glandular tissue that liposuction cannot fully remove

Because glandular tissue is dense and fibrous, liposuction alone often leaves a residual lump behind the nipple, so excision is frequently added for a smooth result.
sections.candidacy:
You may be a candidate if you have persistent enlarged male breast tissue that has not resolved with time, weight loss, or treating any underlying cause.

Good candidates generally:

- Are in stable general health and a stable weight
- Have firm, glandular fullness or fatty fullness that bothers them
- Have realistic expectations about contour and scarring
- Do not smoke, or can stop around surgery to support healing

A Korean surgeon will examine the chest to judge how much is **fat** versus **gland**, which determines the technique.

Because breast enlargement can occasionally stem from medication, hormones, or other medical conditions, a responsible clinic will ask about your history and may request that other causes be ruled out first.

If enlargement is purely from excess body fat (pseudogynecomastia), weight management may be discussed before considering surgery.
sections.procedure_steps:
The procedure is tailored to your chest, but a combined approach commonly follows these stages:

- **Anesthesia** — general anesthesia, or tumescent local anesthesia with sedation for smaller cases
- **Liposuction** — a thin cannula is inserted through tiny incisions to remove excess fat and shape the contour
- **Gland excision** — firm glandular tissue, usually under the nipple, is removed through a small incision, often at the edge of the areola
- **Contouring and closure** — the surgeon checks for symmetry, then closes the small incisions

Many Seoul clinics use **minimal-incision** methods so scars sit discreetly around the areola or in the natural chest crease.

A **compression garment** is applied at the end to control swelling and support the new contour.

Procedures typically take about **1–2 hours**, and most patients go home the same day or after a single night.
sections.recovery_timeline:
Recovery is usually steady, with swelling and tightness easing over several weeks.

- **Days 1–7** — wear the compression vest continuously; mild soreness and swelling are normal; many return to desk work within about a week
- **Weeks 2–3** — sutures are typically removed early; most swelling subsides; light daily activity resumes
- **Weeks 4–6** — many patients return to fuller activity and exercise as cleared by the surgeon
- **Months 3–6** — final contour continues to refine as residual swelling resolves

Clinics commonly advise a **compression garment for roughly one month** to reduce fluid buildup and support healing.

For international patients, surgeons usually want to see you for at least one in-person follow-up before you travel home, then continue check-ins remotely.

Follow your surgeon's specific aftercare instructions, including activity limits and garment use, as guidance varies by case.
sections.cost_breakdown:
Korea's pricing is competitive with many Western markets while access to experienced chest-contouring surgeons is high.

Approximate self-pay ranges reported for Korea:

- **Liposuction only** — about $3,000–$5,500 USD
- **Gland excision** — about $5,500–$7,000 USD
- **Combined liposuction + excision** — about $6,000–$9,000 USD

**Gangnam** clinics may price at the higher end given their premium facilities and international services.

Quotes often bundle the procedure, anesthesia, pre-operative tests, and early follow-ups, but always confirm exactly what is included.

For visiting patients, also budget for flights, accommodation for your stay, interpretation if needed, and any medications or garments.

Korean **National Health Insurance** discounts generally apply only to eligible residents, so most medical travelers should plan for self-pay pricing.
sections.why_korea:
Korea is one of the world's most established destinations for aesthetic and body-contouring surgery, with a deep concentration of experience in **Seoul** and especially **Gangnam**.

Key reasons international patients choose Korea:

- A large base of surgeons performing high volumes of body-contouring procedures
- Clinics that must be **registered to treat foreign patients** and carry malpractice liability insurance
- **Medical Korea**, run by the Korea Health Industry Development Institute (**KHIDI**), as an official information resource for medical travel
- Oversight of medicines and devices by the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (**MFDS**)

Korea's broader medical-tourism infrastructure — international coordinators, interpretation, and aftercare support — is built for visiting patients.

When choosing a clinic, verify that it is legally registered to treat foreign patients and that your surgeon is appropriately qualified, rather than relying on marketing alone.

No surgery is risk-free, so weigh credentials, follow-up access, and clear pricing carefully.

callouts.title: Confirm what your quote includes
callouts.text: Ask whether the price covers anesthesia, pre-operative tests, the compression garment, and follow-up visits. Bundled packages vary widely between Seoul clinics, so an itemized quote helps you compare fairly.

callouts.title: Plan your follow-up before flying
callouts.text: Most surgeons want at least one in-person check, often with suture removal, before you travel home. Building 7–10 days into your trip lets you complete early aftercare in Korea.

keyTakeaways: Gynecomastia surgery in Korea typically combines liposuction with direct gland excision for a flatter chest, with most patients staying about 7–10 days.
Self-pay pricing commonly runs $3,000–$9,000 USD depending on technique; verify clinic registration for foreign patients and confirm exactly what each quote includes.

entities.name: Ministry of Food and Drug Safety

faqs.q: Is gynecomastia surgery in Korea done under general anesthesia?
faqs.a: It can be. Larger or combined cases are often performed under general anesthesia, while smaller corrections may use tumescent local anesthesia with sedation. Your surgeon recommends the safest option based on the extent of tissue to be removed.

faqs.q: How long should I stay in Korea for gynecomastia surgery?
faqs.a: Most international patients plan about 7–10 days. This allows time for pre-operative consultation and tests, the procedure itself, early swelling to settle, suture removal, and at least one in-person follow-up before flying home.

Not always. Liposuction removes excess fat well, but firm glandular tissue behind the nipple often resists it. Many Korean surgeons add direct gland excision in a combined technique to avoid leaving a residual lump and to achieve a smoother contour.

faqs.q: How much does gynecomastia surgery cost in Korea?
faqs.a: Self-pay pricing commonly ranges from about $3,000–$9,000 USD. Liposuction-only cases sit at the lower end, while combined liposuction with gland excision is higher. Gangnam clinics may price toward the top of the range.

faqs.q: When can I exercise again after surgery?
faqs.a: Many patients return to desk work within about a week and resume fuller activity and exercise around 4–6 weeks, as cleared by their surgeon. A compression garment is usually advised for roughly a month to support healing.

Many Seoul clinics use minimal-incision techniques that place scars discreetly around the areola edge or in the natural chest crease. Scarring varies by individual and by how much glandular tissue must be removed.
